bets.consensus_delta: Zip2 was a web-directory for newspapers; web advertising unproven

stake.capital: ~$12M of $22M Zip2 proceeds

bets.thesis: Online banking could be rebuilt from first principles
bets.outcome: X.com merged with Confinity → PayPal → eBay Oct 2002 $1.5B; take ~$170-180M; control lost to board
bets.survival_mechanism: Merger with Confinity (which had the Beaming distribution advantage)

stake.capital: ~$100M into SpaceX + ~$70M into Tesla + ~$10M SolarCity (~all PayPal proceeds)

bets.consensus_delta: Post-dotcom: rockets were billionaire-vanity graveyard (Beal, Kistler, Rotary dead). EVs a 90-year failure category.

bets.outcome: The defining bet. Both companies near-death simultaneously in 2008. Falcon 1's 4th launch succeeds Sep 28 2008 with money for ~1 more attempt. NASA CRS $1.6B Dec 23 2008. Tesla financing closes Christmas Eve 2008.
bets.survival_mechanism: Government as first customer. Controlled inside rounds at the bottom (anti-Jobs). Sequencing: survival before scale.

people.title: Steve Jobs

stake.capital: $1,300 (calculator sale + Wozniak's HP shares)

bets.consensus_delta: Personal computers were hobbyist toys; IBM owned enterprise, nobody owned consumer
bets.enabler: Microprocessor cost curve crossing consumer affordability; Wozniak's engineering
bets.thesis: Personal computer as consumer appliance, not just business tool
bets.outcome: Apple to ~$1B revenue by 1982. Jobs ousted by board and Sculley May 1985 — the canonical control-loss story.
bets.survival_mechanism: Wozniak's technical excellence; venture backing from Markkula

stake.capital: ~$10M into NeXT; ~$10M for Lucasfilm's Computer Graphics group (became Pixar)

bets.consensus_delta: Jobs was a difficult person who failed at Apple; NeXT was dismissed as overpriced Unix
bets.enabler: CGI crossing practical film production threshold; Disney's appetite for animated features
bets.thesis: NeXT: premium workstation + OS. Pixar: CGI film production at commercial quality
bets.outcome: Pixar Disney contract; Toy Story Nov 1995 IPO makes Jobs ~billionaire. Disney acquires Pixar $7.4B (2006). NeXT acquired by Apple $429M (1997).
bets.survival_mechanism: Pixar's Disney contract; Toy Story production revenue paid the bills while NeXT failed commercially

stake.capital: NeXT acquisition; his reputation

bets.consensus_delta: Apple was dying; the right move was selling to Sun or licensing Mac OS
bets.enabler: NeXT's OS (became macOS); design sensibility that had compounded through exile; iMac as beachhead
bets.thesis: Product simplicity + vertical integration = sustainable premium pricing; eliminate SKUs ruthlessly
bets.outcome: Apple from near-bankruptcy to ~$350B market cap at Jobs' death 2011. iPhone (2007) deliberately cannibalized the iPod (the profit engine) — consensus thought him mad.
bets.survival_mechanism: Microsoft's $150M investment (Aug 1997) bought 12 months; iMac 1998 break-even; iPod 2001 cash generation

chain: Apple I/II market validation → personal computer category created
Ousted 1985 → NeXT (failed commercially) + Pixar (succeeded) → exile as forced diversification
Returned with NeXT OS (became macOS) → product simplicity doctrine applied
iMac (1998) → iPod (2001) → iTunes Store (2003) → locked distribution
Deliberate iPod cannibalization (2007, iPhone) → capacitive touch + ARM mobile compute + flash storage crossing viability
App Store 2008 (initially resisted internally) → platform rents at 30%
Vertical integration at every layer → premium pricing sustained post-commoditization

facts: Jobs died Oct 5, 2011. Legacy analysis only.
Apple market cap ~$3.3T (Jun 2026) — the platform rents compound
iPhone revenue still ~50% of Apple; App Store ~$100B gross 2025

future_paths.path: Apple sustains platform rent via regulatory capture until antitrust breaks App Store

future_paths.falsifier: Apple Vision Pro or successor reaches 50M units/yr

people.title: Larry Ellison

stake.capital: ~$2,000 personal savings

bets.consensus_delta: Relational model = academic toy, too slow for real hardware. IBM had the research but wasn't commercializing it.
bets.enabler: CPU/memory cost curves making the 'inefficient' elegant relational model viable — bet on hardware curves rescuing software theory
bets.thesis: Commercialize IBM's own Codd (1970) relational-model research faster than IBM could
bets.outcome: First commercial SQL RDBMS (1979) before IBM. Oracle grows to dominant enterprise database vendor.
bets.survival_mechanism: B2B lock-in via switching costs + maintenance revenue annuity; aggressive sales culture

stake.capital: company survival

bets.consensus_delta: Oracle was dead after the 1990 accounting blowup (stock -80%)
bets.enabler: Existing customer lock-in as moat; switching costs prevented churn despite the crisis
bets.thesis: Maintenance-revenue annuity would survive the crisis; product still best in class
bets.outcome: Oracle survived; rebuilt. Moat: the switching costs meant customers couldn't leave even through the scandal.
bets.survival_mechanism: Maintenance revenue annuity; customers too locked in to switch

stake.capital: Oracle balance sheet; Ellison personal credibility

bets.consensus_delta: Oracle too old/slow to compete in cloud-native AI era; AWS/Azure/GCP had won
bets.enabler: AI capex wave creates demand for any credible large-scale training infrastructure; Oracle's datacenters + networking + contracts
bets.thesis: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure could capture AI training workloads that AWS/Azure were too expensive or constrained to serve
bets.outcome: OpenAI mega-backlog RPO ~$455B (disclosed Sep 2025); stock +~36% in a day; Ellison briefly world's richest. OCI capex ~86% of revenue. Stargate participation. [cf:md — verify RPO number]
bets.survival_mechanism: Existing balance sheet; 40yr enterprise relationships provided Stargate credibility

1990 near-death → survived via lock-in moat → rebuilt

people.title: Jensen Huang

stake.capital: $2M seed

bets.consensus_delta: 3D graphics was a niche; no one would buy a graphics chip from a startup with no semiconductor experience
bets.enabler: Nintendo/Sega 3D gaming boom incoming; polygon-based 3D crossing viability
bets.thesis: 3D graphics would mass-market; build a dedicated chip architecture for it
bets.outcome: NV1 failure (1995-96) nearly killed the company; NV3/RIVA 128 (1997) saved it; GeForce 256 (1999) established dominance
bets.survival_mechanism: Pivoted architecture after NV1; deal with SEGA for Saturn/Dreamcast kept cash flowing

stake.capital: Years of GPU profit margin; NVIDIA's goodwill with shareholders

bets.consensus_delta: General-purpose GPU computing had no market; scientists would just use CPUs; no one would learn CUDA
bets.enabler: Dennard scaling ended (2005-07); parallel compute becoming the only path; neural net research beginning
bets.thesis: Invest years of GPU margin into a general-purpose programming layer before any market demanded it — CUDA
bets.outcome: CUDA (2007) + decade of academic/research adoption with zero consumer demand. When AlexNet (2012) hit, CUDA was the only ergonomic path. 15yr moat established.
bets.survival_mechanism: Gaming division profitability funded the CUDA decade; hardware sold even without CUDA adoption

people.title: Morris Chang

stake.capital: Reputation + late career: founded TSMC at 56 after being passed over for TI CEO
stake.reputation: respected TI exec betting on an unproven business model

bets.consensus_delta: Every serious chipmaker designed AND manufactured (IDM model). A fab that only made other people's chips was considered a contract shop with no future — Intel and TI saw no threat.
bets.enabler: Taiwan government industrial policy (ITRI) seeking a flagship; process technology licensable from Philips; rising mask costs starting to strain small design houses
bets.thesis: Separate design from manufacturing. A pure-play foundry that never competes with its customers can aggregate everyone's volume and win on process scale.
bets.outcome: TSMC created the fabless industry as a side effect: NVIDIA, Qualcomm, Broadcom, Apple silicon all exist because the foundry exists. TSMC became the indispensable node of the entire digital economy.
bets.survival_mechanism: Government anchor funding; never competing with customers meant every fabless win was a TSMC win

stake.capital: Returned as CEO at 77 during financial crisis; doubled capex into the downturn

bets.consensus_delta: 2009 consensus: cut capex, survive the crisis. Chang fired his successor and raised capex ~2x into the worst demand year in memory.
bets.enabler: Smartphone wave visible in design pipelines 2 years before revenue; 28nm node transition approaching
bets.thesis: Mobile silicon demand would make 28nm the biggest node ever; whoever had capacity ready would take everything
bets.outcome: TSMC's 28nm dominance (~90%+ share at peak) funded the leading-edge treadmill that competitors then couldn't afford to ride. GlobalFoundries quit at 7nm (2018); only Samsung remains, behind.
bets.survival_mechanism: Foundry model's customer diversification — no single end-market crash could kill the order book

chain: TI passes Chang over for CEO → Taiwan's ITRI recruits him → TSMC founded 1987
Pure-play foundry → fabless startups become viable → NVIDIA (1993) can exist without a fab
Customer-aggregated volume → process leadership → mask/EUV costs become a moat only TSMC can amortize
2009 capex double-down → 28nm mobile dominance → Apple A-series sole-source
AI wave 2023→ every frontier chip (NVIDIA, AMD, custom silicon) funnels through TSMC advanced nodes + CoWoS packaging
Single point of failure for the digital economy, sitting 110 miles from China

facts: Retired 2018; legacy structure intact under Liu/Wei leadership
TSMC >60% global foundry share, >90% at leading edge
Arizona fabs ramping (N4 yields reported at Taiwan parity 2025, cf:md); Japan/Germany fabs in progress
CoWoS advanced packaging the binding constraint of the AI build-out 2023-25

future_paths.path: Geographic diversification succeeds; TSMC becomes a distributed utility and the Taiwan discount narrows
future_paths.falsifier: Overseas fabs stuck >20% cost premium or trailing-node-only through 2028

future_paths.path: Taiwan-strait crisis forces emergency re-architecture of global supply chain

dead_bodies.name: UMC
dead_bodies.why: Taiwan's other foundry; kept a hybrid model and own-brand ambitions early; permanently a node behind — the half-commitment counterfactual

dead_bodies.name: GlobalFoundries
dead_bodies.why: AMD's spun-off fabs; abandoned 7nm in 2018 — proof the capex treadmill kills everyone who isn't aggregating the whole industry's volume

dead_bodies.name: SMIC
dead_bodies.why: State-backed follower; export controls capped it at trailing edge — shows the model is replicable but the ecosystem position is not

lessons: The biggest bets are business-model bets, not technology bets — TSMC used licensed process tech
Founded at 56: the late-career founder with full domain knowledge is an underpriced archetype
Never compete with your customers is a structural moat disguised as a courtesy
Counter-cyclical capex (2009) is the foundry version of buying the bottom — physical capacity is the slowest variable

stake.capital: $20M into Alibaba (after 5-minute meeting with Jack Ma)

bets.survival_mechanism: Small absolute check; SoftBank's telecom cash flows carried the holding period

stake.capital: $15B+ LBO of Vodafone Japan — largest in Japanese history; bet-the-company leverage

bets.consensus_delta: Vodafone Japan was a failing #3 carrier; SoftBank had no telecom experience and had just lost more money than any human in history (-$70B personal in dot-com crash)
bets.enabler: Secret: Son met Jobs pre-iPhone and secured Japan exclusivity on a phone that didn't exist yet
bets.thesis: Mobile internet would obsolete carrier hierarchies; an exclusive on the device that causes the shift resets the market
bets.outcome: iPhone exclusivity 2008 transformed SoftBank Mobile into the growth carrier; LBO paid off and funded Vision Fund era
bets.survival_mechanism: Japanese bank relationships willing to roll debt; carrier cash flows are recession-proof

stake.capital: $32B Arm acquisition + $100B Vision Fund (Saudi PIF $45B anchor)
stake.reputation: the man who lost the most money ever, redeemed once

bets.consensus_delta: Consensus was actually WITH him on tech, against him on price discipline — the contrarian element was scale and speed of deployment, which proved to be the flaw
bets.enabler: ZIRP: $100B of near-free capital seeking duration
bets.thesis: Information revolution singularity; own the chokepoints (Arm) and spray the application layer
bets.outcome: WeWork -$14B+ (the canonical governance failure), Katerra/Zume/Wirecard zeros — but Arm 2023 IPO + holdings recovered the fund era. Net: survived his own excess.
bets.survival_mechanism: Arm as the hard asset under the speculative book; ability to sell Alibaba in tranches to cover margin

stake.capital: $30B+ committed to OpenAI; Stargate datacenter JV; Ampere acquisition

bets.consensus_delta: This time he is the consensus — buying the most agreed-upon asset at the top of the most agreed-upon cycle. The bet is on his own pattern inverting.
bets.enabler: AI capex supercycle; OpenAI's need for non-Microsoft capital
bets.thesis: ASI is the final platform; the only error is under-allocation
bets.outcome: Open. SoftBank sold its entire NVIDIA stake (Oct 2025) to fund it — all-in, again.
bets.survival_mechanism: Arm equity as collateral; Japanese rates still low

Crash: -96%, -$70B — survives via telecom cash flows (the lesson others read as luck, he read as license)

Vodafone Japan LBO + secret iPhone exclusivity → redemption
ZIRP + Saudi capital → Vision Fund: the pattern at toxic scale → WeWork
Arm IPO recovery → 2025: everything into OpenAI/Stargate — variance maximalism, final act

Sold entire NVIDIA position (~$5.8B, Oct 2025) to fund AI commitments
Arm ~90% owned — the hard asset; Ampere acquired 2025

dead_bodies.why: -$14B+; governance abdication at scale — capital as a substitute for judgment. Neumann walked with ~$1B+; LPs ate the loss

dead_bodies.name: Katerra
dead_bodies.why: $2B+ into construction-tech rollup; bankrupt 2021 — capital cannot compress industries whose constraint is physics and permits

dead_bodies.name: Zume Pizza
dead_bodies.why: $375M for pizza robots; the Vision Fund check size forcing startups into premature scale was the actual killer

dead_bodies.name: FTX stake
dead_bodies.why: $100M written to zero in days — diligence speed matched to deployment speed

lessons: One Alibaba pays for everything: power-law internalized as strategy — but only with a cash-flow engine that survives the drawdowns
The Vodafone/iPhone move is the masterpiece: secure the exclusive on the thing that resets the market before it exists
Vision Fund is the cautionary inversion: the same conviction, deployed as consensus at scale, destroys alpha
His survival mechanism was never the bets — it was Japanese banking relationships and a hard asset he refused to sell

people.title: Jeff Bezos

stake.capital: Left D.E. Shaw SVP track; $10K founding + parents' $245K life savings
stake.reputation: quant walking away from a sure career

bets.consensus_delta: Web retail in 1994 was a hobbyist curiosity; the regret-minimization framework — judge the decision at 80, not at 35 — was the actual innovation
bets.enabler: Web growing 2300%/yr; books as the perfect wedge (infinite catalog, standard product, shippable)
bets.thesis: Start with books, become the everything store; obsess over customer, not competitor
bets.outcome: Survived the dot-com crash that killed every comparable (Webvan, Pets.com) via 2000 convertible raise weeks before the window shut
bets.survival_mechanism: Raised $672M convertible debt Feb 2000 — one month before the crash closed capital markets. Timing of the raise, not the model, was survival.

stake.capital: Years of negative free cash flow into internal infrastructure during post-crash winter

bets.consensus_delta: A retailer selling compute? Wall Street saw a distraction. Competitors (Google, Microsoft) had the tech and didn't ship it for 4+ years — the consensus blindness was industry-wide
bets.enabler: Internal need: Amazon's own infrastructure pain produced the product. Hardened APIs as company policy (the 2002 mandate)
bets.thesis: Compute as metered utility; developers will rent infrastructure the way they consume electricity
bets.outcome: AWS launched 2006; created the cloud category; still ~60% of Amazon operating income. Precondition for both the SaaS decade and the AI training era.
bets.survival_mechanism: Retail cash flows funded the build; no external capital needed meant no one could vote it down

stake.capital: ~$1B/yr personal Amazon stock sales into Blue Origin
stake.reputation: secondary to Musk in the public narrative

bets.consensus_delta: Gradatim Ferociter (step by step, ferociously) vs SpaceX's iterate-by-explosion; consensus now says the tortoise lost the decade
bets.enabler: Personal wealth removing all funding risk — and all funding discipline
bets.thesis: Millions of people living and working in space; move heavy industry off-Earth
bets.outcome: New Glenn flew 2025; Blue lags SpaceX by ~8 years in cadence. The control case for whether external pressure is a feature.
bets.survival_mechanism: Infinite personal funding — survival guaranteed, urgency forfeited

chain: D.E. Shaw quant sees web growth stats → regret-minimization → books wedge 1994
Everything-store flywheel: selection → traffic → sellers → selection
Feb 2000: $672M convertible raised weeks before the window shut — survives the extinction event
Internal infra pain + 2002 API mandate → AWS 2006 → cloud category created
AWS = precondition: ZIRP SaaS decade runs on it; AI training era rents from it
Prime + logistics capex moat → retail margin structurally thin but unassailable
2021 steps back; Blue Origin and Washington Post as the post-CEO portfolio

facts: Executive chairman; sells ~$5-10B/yr for Blue Origin and philanthropy
AWS ~$110B+ run rate, facing genuine AI-era competition (Azure/OpenAI gravity) for the first time

Amazon capex guided to ~$200B 2026, mostly AI infrastructure (cf:md)

future_paths.falsifier: Fulfillment cost per unit flat or rising through 2027

dead_bodies.why: $800M+ burned on grocery delivery 1999-2001 — the same thesis Amazon Fresh later won, a decade early on infrastructure that didn't exist; right idea, wrong slow variables

dead_bodies.name: Pets.com
dead_bodies.why: The mascot of dot-com excess; unit economics negative at any scale — proximity to a good thesis (online retail) is not the thesis

dead_bodies.name: Fire Phone
dead_bodies.why: Amazon's own $170M writeoff — proof the flywheel doesn't transfer to hardware ego projects; institutionalized as 'failure is fine' lore

dead_bodies.name: Kozmo
dead_bodies.why: 1-hour delivery 1998 — the Prime Now thesis, 17 years early; timing IS the thesis

lessons: Regret-minimization is a decision algorithm for irreversible bets — judge from the deathbed, not the quarter
The Feb 2000 raise: survival is a capital-markets timing skill, separate from operating skill
AWS pattern: your internal pain, productized, is the highest-conviction startup — the customer already exists (you)
Blue Origin counterfactual: unlimited funding without external forcing is how the tortoise stays a tortoise

people.title: Peter Thiel

stake.capital: Founded Confinity (→PayPal) post-law-clerk career swerve
stake.control: shared, then merged with Musk's X.com

bets.consensus_delta: Digital payments needed banks; fraud would eat any startup alive
bets.enabler: eBay's seller base needed payments the banks wouldn't build; email as the rail
bets.thesis: New currency-transfer layer on the internet, outside the banking system
bets.outcome: eBay acquisition Oct 2002 $1.5B; Thiel's ~$55M became the seed of everything after. The 'PayPal Mafia' as the real asset.
bets.survival_mechanism: Fraud-detection tooling (Gausebeck-Levchin) as the actual moat; merger with X.com consolidated the category

stake.capital: $500K into Facebook for 10.2% — first outside check

bets.consensus_delta: Friendster and MySpace already 'won' social; a college directory was a feature, not a company
bets.enabler: Real-identity graph (college email verification) — the structural difference everyone else missed
bets.thesis: Network effects on verified identity beat scale on pseudonymous identity
bets.outcome: >$1B return on $500K — possibly the best angel check ever written. Sold most at IPO (the exit discipline nobody copies).
bets.survival_mechanism: Check sized to lose; asymmetry did all the work

stake.capital: Co-founded Palantir; personal capital + Founders Fund when VCs refused for years
stake.reputation: staked on an unfashionable customer: the state

bets.consensus_delta: Post-9/11 VCs wouldn't touch government sales: long cycles, low margins, ethical heat. Silicon Valley's institutional allergy to the state was the arbitrage
bets.enabler: In-Q-Tel (CIA venture arm) as first money; intelligence community's data-integration pain
bets.thesis: Government is the highest-value underserved software customer; whoever tolerates the sales cycle owns it for decades
bets.outcome: IPO 2020; defense-AI rerating 2023-25 made it a top-20 US company by market cap (cf:md). Anduril (FF-backed) repeated the playbook in hardware.
bets.survival_mechanism: Thiel's personal capital bridged a decade VCs wouldn't fund; gov contracts, once won, are annuities

chain: PayPal $55M + the Mafia network → Founders Fund + the angel network that seeded Web 2.0
Facebook $500K → $1B+: asymmetry as doctrine ('what important truth do few agree with you on?')
Palantir: government-as-customer when the Valley refused — same move as Musk's NASA, applied to software
Zero to One codifies it: competition is for losers, monopoly via secrets
Anduril/defense-tech wave 2017→: his unfashionable-customer thesis becomes the consensus a decade later
Political capital deployment 2016/2024 — the same contrarian sizing applied outside markets (cf:md on returns)

facts: Founders Fund: defense/hard-tech tilt fully vindicated by 2024-26 procurement shift
Palantir among the most valuable software companies; Anduril valued ~$30B+ (cf:md)
Sold most Palantir personal stake in tranches 2024-25 (cf:md) — exit discipline pattern repeating
The 'contrarian truth' question now taught in every accelerator — the edge is crowded

future_paths.path: Defense-tech window matures; Anduril IPOs as the new prime
future_paths.falsifier: Anduril fails to win a program-of-record >$1B by 2027

future_paths.falsifier: Revenue growth sustains >30% YoY with expanding FCF through 2027

future_paths.path: His political-capital bets convert into durable regulatory advantage for portfolio
future_paths.falsifier: No major portfolio company wins sole-source federal awards at scale

dead_bodies.name: Clarium Capital
dead_bodies.why: His macro hedge fund: peaked ~$8B, right theses (oil, dollar) with wrong timing and sizing — proof that contrarian-truth doctrine does not transfer to liquid markets where timing is the whole game

dead_bodies.name: Friendster/MySpace
dead_bodies.why: The consensus social winners his Facebook check bet against — scale without verified identity was the dead end

dead_bodies.name: The VCs who passed on Palantir for a decade
dead_bodies.why: Not a company death but a category death: an entire asset class structurally unable to underwrite the state as customer

lessons: Size the check to the asymmetry, not the conviction: $500K/10% beats $50M/1% on the same insight
Unfashionable customers (government) are mispriced for structural reasons that persist for decades
Clarium is the honest record: the same brain lost in liquid markets — edge is domain-specific
Exit discipline: he sold Facebook at IPO and Palantir into the rerating — contrarian on the way out too

people.title: Warren Buffett

stake.capital: $1B into Coca-Cola — ~25% of Berkshire's book value
stake.reputation: value investor buying a 'fully priced' brand

bets.consensus_delta: Post-Black-Monday 1987; Graham-school value orthodoxy said KO was too expensive. Buying quality at fair price over fair at cheap price was Munger's heresy, executed at conviction size
bets.enabler: Crash gave the entry; float from insurance operations gave permanent capital that could never be margin-called
bets.thesis: A durable consumer monopoly compounds untouched for decades; the holding period IS the edge
bets.outcome: The template position: ~16x plus dividends; the public proof that inactivity outcompounds activity
bets.survival_mechanism: Insurance float = leverage with no margin calls; the structure makes panic-selling impossible

stake.capital: $20B+ deployed into Goldman/GE/BAC preferreds at the bottom
stake.reputation: the last credible counterparty on earth

bets.consensus_delta: October 2008: consensus was systemic collapse. He sold the put on American capitalism, in public, with terms only he could command (10% coupons + warrants)
bets.enabler: 50 years of hoarded credibility + cash — the bet was prepared for decades before the moment existed
bets.thesis: Be greedy when others are fearful — executed as a structural advantage (his signature WAS the collateral)
bets.outcome: ~$10B+ profit on the crisis deals; the reputational annuity compounded further
bets.survival_mechanism: Cash hoard criticized as drag for years was the option premium for this exact moment

stake.capital: ~$36B into Apple (peak position ~$170B+) — largest position in Berkshire history
stake.reputation: the man who swore off tech for 50 years

bets.consensus_delta: Mild — Apple was already loved. The contrarian act was internal: breaking his own circle-of-competence rule when the facts changed (iPhone = consumer staple with switching costs, not tech)
bets.enabler: Recategorization: Apple as Coca-Cola with a screen. The insight was taxonomic, not technical
bets.thesis: Ecosystem lock-in + buybacks = a consumer monopoly wearing a tech costume
bets.outcome: ~$100B+ gain — the best single position in firm history, made past age 85
bets.survival_mechanism: Sized within float structure; trimmed 2024-25 into strength (cf:hi)

chain: Graham apprenticeship → cigar-butt value → Munger converts him to quality-at-scale
Insurance float (National Indemnity 1967) → permanent leverage no one can call — the structural invention everything rests on
Coca-Cola 1988 → the hold-forever template → 'our favorite holding period is forever'
Avoided dot-com entirely → mocked in 1999 ('lost his touch') → vindicated by 2002
2008: the prepared opportunist — credibility itself as the asset deployed
Apple 2016: rule-breaking at 86 when the categorization changed — the system updated, the discipline didn't
May 2025: announces handover; Abel CEO Jan 2026 — the 60-year compounding run ends on schedule

facts: Stepped down as CEO end-2025; Greg Abel operating, Buffett remains chairman (cf:hi)
Berkshire cash position ~$350B at handover — the option premium at maximum, market at highs (cf:md)
Trimmed Apple substantially 2024-25; never bought NVIDIA or any AI name
The missing-the-AI-wave critique is identical in structure to the 1999 critique — resolution pending

future_paths.falsifier: BRK matches or beats SPX over Abel's first 3 years

future_paths.falsifier: Cash still >$300B with no major deployment after a >25% market drawdown

future_paths.path: The no-AI abstention is vindicated as the 1999 call repeated
future_paths.falsifier: AI capex translates into durable >15% FCF growth at hyperscalers through 2028

dead_bodies.why: Paid in Berkshire stock (1993) for a company that went to zero — his self-declared worst deal: the error wasn't the asset, it was the currency (~$10B+ in foregone BRK)

dead_bodies.name: IBM
dead_bodies.why: The failed first tech exception (2011-17): bought the incumbent narrative, exited flat — proof his Apple insight was taxonomy, not tech competence

dead_bodies.name: Berkshire Hathaway itself (textile mill)
dead_bodies.why: The original purchase was a value trap he ran for 20 years out of discipline-turned-stubbornness — named the whole vehicle after his worst habit as a reminder

dead_bodies.name: LTCM (the counter-ghost)
dead_bodies.why: Genius + leverage + illiquidity, 1998. The fund he almost bought at the bottom and didn't need to — the anti-Berkshire: brilliance with margin calls

lessons: The anti-bet IS a bet: 50 years of refusing what you don't understand is a position with carry costs, and he paid them publicly in 1999 and 2023-25
Float is the masterstroke: permanent leverage without margin calls — structure beats stock-picking
The 2008 trades were 50 years in preparation: hoarded credibility + cash = an option nobody else could write
Apple proves the system can update without breaking: the rule was never 'no tech,' it was 'no unknowable economics'

people.title: Zhang Yiming

stake.capital: Founded ByteDance after 4 prior startups; SIG + Yuri Milner early backing

bets.consensus_delta: 2012 consensus: distribution = search (Baidu) or social graph (Tencent). A recommendation-first feed with no social graph and no search was considered a content farm
bets.enabler: Smartphone penetration in China crossing 50%; ML recommendation systems maturing; incumbents structurally unable to cannibalize their graph/search cash cows
bets.thesis: The algorithm IS the distribution: machine-learned interest graphs beat declared social graphs at capturing attention
bets.outcome: Toutiao → Douyin (2016) → Musical.ly acquisition $1B (2017) → TikTok: the largest attention arbitrage in history; first Chinese consumer product to win globally
bets.survival_mechanism: Multiple product shots on the same algorithmic engine; revenue from Chinese ads funded the global land grab

stake.capital: Stepped down as CEO at 38 at the peak

bets.consensus_delta: Founders don't quit at the top. But Jack Ma's 2020 disappearance had just demonstrated what visible founder-power costs under Beijing's crackdown
bets.enabler: Reading the regulatory regime change one cycle ahead — the same pattern-recognition applied to politics
bets.thesis: In a state-power regime shift, the founder's visibility is a liability to the asset; step back before being stepped on
bets.outcome: ByteDance survived the crackdown intact while Alibaba/Didi/Ant were dismembered. Net worth ~$45B+ — richest Chinese founder (cf:md)
bets.survival_mechanism: The resignation WAS the mechanism

chain: 4 failed/small startups → pattern: distribution is the game, content is fungible
Toutiao 2012: recommendation beats search/social for attention → engagement metrics incumbents can't match
Douyin 2016 + Musical.ly 2017: same engine, video form factor → TikTok global
US growth → CFIUS scrutiny 2019 → divest-or-ban law 2024 → US JV restructuring 2025 (Oracle/Silver Lake/MGX, cf:md)
2021: steps down ahead of crackdown — survives what dismembered Alibaba/Ant/Didi
The algorithm itself becomes contested sovereign asset: export-controlled by China, demanded by US — the first time a recommendation model is treated as strategic technology

facts: ByteDance revenue >$150B (2024, cf:md) — rivals Meta; still private
TikTok US restructured into JV with US investors holding majority, algorithm licensed under Chinese export approval (cf:md)
Zhang largest individual shareholder ~20%; no operational role; based partly in Singapore
Doubao/AI push: ByteDance among the largest AI capex spenders in China (cf:md)

future_paths.falsifier: Still private with no filed prospectus by end 2028

dead_bodies.why: Short video 4 years before Douyin, shut down 2017 by Twitter — the same product without a recommendation engine or monetization patience; form factor was never the moat

dead_bodies.name: Quibi
dead_bodies.why: $1.75B for premium short video 2020 — content-first, algorithm-absent; died in 6 months while TikTok grew through the same pandemic

dead_bodies.name: Renren/Kaixin001
dead_bodies.why: China's Facebook clones — bet the social graph in the market where the interest graph won

dead_bodies.name: Jack Ma (as cautionary trajectory)
dead_bodies.why: Not dead but dismembered: Ant IPO cancelled 48hr before listing, founder vanished from public life — the visible-founder cost Zhang's 2021 resignation was priced against

lessons: The interest graph beats the social graph — declared preferences lie, behavior doesn't; this is now every feed's architecture
Acquiring Musical.ly for its user base and porting the algorithm is the cleanest tech-transfer acquisition ever executed
Regulatory regime change is a slow variable readable years ahead: Zhang read 2020-21 Beijing correctly and traded title for survival
Attention is a commodity market with network effects — the first truly global Chinese consumer win came from the most culturally neutral asset: an algorithm

people.title: Demis Hassabis

stake.capital: Founded DeepMind when 'AGI' was academic career suicide
stake.reputation: games prodigy + neuroscience PhD betting both on a fringe goal

bets.consensus_delta: 2010: 'AGI' was a crank word. Serious ML people did narrow applications; saying 'general intelligence' out loud cost you funding and tenure. Thiel's Founders Fund wrote the first check precisely because it was unfundable elsewhere
bets.enabler: Deep learning's quiet pre-2012 progress; games as a legible benchmark ladder (Atari → Go); neuroscience framing as differentiation
bets.thesis: Intelligence is learnable by general algorithms; games provide the gradient of increasingly hard, measurable environments
bets.outcome: Atari DQN 2013 → the demo that triggered the 2014 acquisition bidding
bets.survival_mechanism: Thiel/Musk angel capital; London location (cheaper, defensible talent pool, away from Valley poaching)

stake.capital: Sold to Google for ~$650M — pre-revenue, pre-product
stake.reputation: sold out the mission, said critics

bets.consensus_delta: Founders don't sell the mission pre-product. But Hassabis priced what nobody else had: AGI research would need corporate-scale compute YEARS before scaling laws made this consensus (Kaplan 2020)
bets.enabler: Google's compute + Facebook's competing bid driving terms
bets.thesis: The mission requires compute that only a hyperscaler can provide; sell the company, keep the research agenda
bets.outcome: AlphaGo 2016 (the public shock), AlphaZero, AlphaFold 2020 — the compute-access trade paid in full
bets.survival_mechanism: Ethics board + autonomy terms; being in London kept operational independence from Mountain View

stake.capital: Years of DeepMind credibility into protein folding — a 50-year grand challenge

bets.consensus_delta: Structural biology consensus: folding was decades away; an ML team without wet-lab pedigree winning CASP was considered absurd
bets.enabler: Transformer-era architectures + PDB's 50 years of open structural data + games-honed engineering culture
bets.thesis: AI does novel science, not just games — folding is the proof case with maximal scientific legibility
bets.outcome: CASP14 2020 effectively solved the problem; 200M+ structures released free; Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 — the first AI-native Nobel
bets.survival_mechanism: Google's patience capital; the games track record bought internal license for a moonshot

chain: Chess master at 13 → game design (Theme Park at 17) → neuroscience PhD: the intelligence obsession runs the whole arc
DeepMind 2010 with games-as-benchmark thesis → Atari DQN → Google acquisition 2014
The compute-access trade: sold equity, kept agenda — 6 years before scaling laws made the logic public
AlphaGo beats Lee Sedol 2016: 280M Chinese viewers; the event that triggered China's national AI strategy — a geopolitical chain-reaction from a board game
AlphaFold 2020 → Nobel 2024: AI does novel science, the thesis vindicated at the highest level
2023: merged with Google Brain into Google DeepMind, Hassabis runs all of Google's AI — the acquired founder now commands the acquirer's frontier effort
Isomorphic Labs spinout: folding → drug discovery, the commercial second act

facts: CEO Google DeepMind: Gemini line is Google's frontier bet under his command
Nobel Chemistry 2024 (with Jumper) for AlphaFold
Isomorphic Labs raised $600M (2025); pharma partnerships with Lilly/Novartis (cf:hi)
Runs the only frontier lab inside a company that owns its full stack: TPUs, data, distribution

future_paths.path: Isomorphic delivers an AI-designed drug candidate into Phase 2 — the AlphaFold-to-medicine chain completes
future_paths.falsifier: No Isomorphic-originated candidate in human trials by end 2028

people.title: Ilya Sutskever

stake.capital: PhD years on neural nets through the winter when the field had moved on
stake.reputation: Hinton's student in a dismissed subfield

bets.consensus_delta: 2000s ML consensus: neural nets were a dead end — SVMs and graphical models won the benchmarks and the citations
bets.enabler: GPUs crossing the throughput threshold; ImageNet providing the scoreboard; Hinton's lab as the last redoubt
bets.thesis: Neural networks fail only for scale reasons; with enough compute and data they win everything
bets.outcome: AlexNet (with Krizhevsky, under Hinton): top-5 error 26.2%→15.3% — the discontinuity that started the modern era. Google acquires the 3-person 'company' for ~$44M
bets.survival_mechanism: Academic shelter (Hinton's lab, CIFAR funding) through the winter

stake.capital: Left Google Brain's certainty for OpenAI co-founder/chief scientist
stake.reputation: the AlexNet name lent the nonprofit its scientific credibility

bets.consensus_delta: Leaving Google's resources for a nonprofit was considered career mismanagement; 'AGI within decades' was still a fringe position even post-AlexNet
bets.enabler: Musk/Altman capital; the scaling intuition that bigger was not yet priced in anywhere
bets.thesis: Scale is the algorithm: predict-the-next-token at sufficient size yields general capability
bets.outcome: GPT line under his research direction: GPT-2→3→4 — the scaling conviction became the industry's organizing principle
bets.survival_mechanism: Microsoft capital (2019) arrived when the nonprofit structure hit its funding ceiling

stake.capital: Joined the board action removing Altman — Nov 2023

bets.consensus_delta: The governance structure said the board could fire the CEO; the capital structure (Microsoft, employees' equity) said otherwise. He bet on paper authority against money gravity
bets.enabler: Nonprofit board's formal power — which proved to be the fiction
bets.thesis: Safety governance should bind commercial velocity when they conflict
bets.outcome: Total reversal in 72 hours: 95% of employees threatened to walk, Altman returned, Sutskever recanted publicly, board dissolved. The clearest natural experiment ever run on where power actually lives in an AI lab
bets.survival_mechanism: None — the failed bet; he left within months

stake.capital: Founded SSI (Safe Superintelligence Inc) — no product, no revenue, by design
stake.reputation: the last pure conviction asset in the field

bets.consensus_delta: A lab that pledges to ship NOTHING until superintelligence is safe — no API, no products, no interim revenue — is either the only honest position or the largest tuition payment in venture history
bets.enabler: His name alone: $1B at founding, ~$30B+ valuation by 2025 (cf:md) with zero disclosed work
bets.thesis: Straight-shot superintelligence: skip the product treadmill entirely; one team, one goal, no commercial distraction
bets.outcome: Open. The purest experiment in conviction-as-asset ever run
bets.survival_mechanism: Burn discipline + investors who underwrote the no-product pledge knowingly

people.title: Dario Amodei

stake.capital: Left Google for OpenAI to lead safety+scaling research

bets.consensus_delta: Empirical scaling research was unglamorous engineering when the field chased architectures; 'predictable capability from compute' sounded like an accounting exercise, not science
bets.enabler: OpenAI's compute commitment; GPT-1/2 as the testbed
bets.thesis: Capability is a smooth, predictable function of compute, data, and parameters — measure it, then bet on the curve
bets.outcome: GPT-2/GPT-3 scaling work (with Kaplan et al. 2020): the paper class that made $100B datacenters underwritable — you cannot finance a miracle, you can finance a power law
bets.survival_mechanism: The results were immediately load-bearing for the org's fundraising — indispensability as job security

stake.capital: Left OpenAI with 6 senior colleagues incl. sister Daniela to found Anthropic
stake.reputation: walking out of the leading lab on a disagreement
stake.control: CEO, with structure (LTBT) designed from his Nov-2023-avoidant priors

bets.consensus_delta: 'A safety-focused lab' read as a niche nonprofit play; the consensus was that being second-mover in frontier models with LESS capital was structurally doomed
bets.enabler: The scaling laws he co-wrote: he knew exactly what compute would buy; safety differentiation as enterprise positioning was unpriced
bets.thesis: Safety and capability are complements at the frontier — enterprises will pay for the trustworthy frontier model; constraint is a moat
bets.outcome: Claude line; ~$1B → ~$30B ARR in roughly 15 months (2025-26, cf:md) — fastest enterprise software ramp on record, dominated by coding
bets.survival_mechanism: Google then Amazon ($8B) compute deals — renting the hyperscaler rivalry instead of being owned by one side

stake.capital: Bet the company on code: Claude optimized for software engineering over consumer chat

bets.consensus_delta: Consumer chat was the visible market (ChatGPT's 100M users); coding looked like a feature, not the wedge. It was the wedge: highest willingness-to-pay, clearest eval, fastest feedback loop
bets.enabler: Code is the one domain where output is machine-verifiable — RL on verifiable rewards compounds fastest exactly there
bets.thesis: The first durable AI labor market is software labor; win the developers and the enterprise follows
bets.outcome: Claude Code + API coding dominance drove the ARR ramp; competitor labs reoriented to follow (cf:md)
bets.survival_mechanism: Enterprise contracts = revenue durability consumer chat never had

'Machines of Loving Grace' + public compute-curve essays: the founder as the field's macro forecaster

Pick the domain where output is verifiable (code): RL compounds fastest where reward is cleanest — domain selection beat model superiority

people.title: Lisa Su

stake.capital: Took AMD CEO with stock ~$2 and bankruptcy rumors priced in
stake.reputation: IBM-pedigree engineer taking the industry's poisoned chalice

bets.consensus_delta: 'AMD is structurally dead' was consensus: x86 duopoly loser, fabs sold, console-margin business, $2.2B debt. The job itself was considered career-ending
bets.enabler: Intel's coming process stumbles (10nm) were not yet visible; TSMC's rise meant fabless AMD could ride someone else's process leadership for the first time
bets.thesis: One great architecture (Zen), no hedges, chiplet economics: small dies = yield advantage that monolithic Intel cannot structurally match
bets.outcome: Zen 2017 → datacenter share from ~0% to >30%; stock ~$2 → ~50-100x over the decade; Intel — the 'safe' giant — became the cautionary tale instead
bets.survival_mechanism: Console semi-custom revenue (Sony/Microsoft) as the cash bridge through the 3-year Zen development valley

stake.capital: $49B all-stock Xilinx acquisition — largest semi deal then closed

bets.consensus_delta: Mild contrarianism — FPGA+CPU synergy was questioned but not ridiculed
bets.enabler: Stock-as-currency after the rerating; data-center heterogeneous-compute thesis
bets.thesis: Adaptive computing (FPGA) + CPU + GPU under one roof = the full datacenter silicon menu
bets.outcome: Embedded segment cushioned the 2022-23 PC downturn; AI era made the full-menu thesis more valuable
bets.survival_mechanism: All-stock structure — no leverage taken into the cycle

stake.capital: MI300/350/400 line + ROCm software push against CUDA's 15-year moat
stake.reputation: the second act: from beating a stumbling Intel to attacking a peaking NVIDIA

bets.consensus_delta: Consensus: CUDA lock-in is unbreakable; AMD is the permanent #2 taking scraps. The bet: at $200B+/yr of AI capex, customers will FUND a second source into existence
bets.enabler: Hyperscalers' monopsony fear of NVIDIA; PyTorch/Triton abstraction layers eroding CUDA's API lock; OpenAI/Oracle multi-GW AMD commitments 2025 (cf:md)
bets.thesis: Buyers with $100B budgets do not tolerate a sole supplier; be the credible alternative when the dam breaks
bets.outcome: Open: MI-line revenue multi-billion and growing; OpenAI warrant deal 2025 aligned the biggest customer with AMD equity upside (cf:md)
bets.survival_mechanism: CPU/embedded businesses fund the GPU assault indefinitely — she can lose for years and stay

chain: MIT PhD (SOI research) → IBM (copper interconnect era) → Freescale → AMD 2012, CEO 2014
Zen bet: one architecture, no hedge, console cash as the bridge
Chiplets: yield economics as architecture — the structural advantage Intel's monolithic dies couldn't answer
Intel's 10nm/7nm stumbles open the door exactly as Zen matures: prepared luck
Datacenter share 0→30%+; the $2 stock becomes a top-10 semi company
Xilinx 2022 → full-menu silicon → AI era: MI-line + ROCm assault on CUDA
2025: OpenAI/Oracle gigawatt-scale AMD commitments — customers funding the second source into existence (cf:md)

facts: MI400 generation shipping; AI accelerator revenue multi-billion/yr but still <15% of NVIDIA's (cf:md)
OpenAI warrant structure: customer equity-aligned with AMD's success (cf:md)
ROCm maturity remains the honest gap vs CUDA — software, not silicon, is the moat being assaulted
Named among likely Intel-crisis beneficiaries as US pushes second-source policy (cf:lo)

future_paths.falsifier: MI-line share still <10% of accelerator revenue exiting 2027

future_paths.path: CUDA moat erodes via compiler/framework abstraction (PyTorch/Triton/MLIR) faster than expected
future_paths.falsifier: ROCm-targeted workloads remain <15% of open-framework deployments through 2027

dead_bodies.why: Dataflow AI-chip pioneer, bankrupt 2020 — first-mover in AI silicon means nothing without a customer bridge to survive the development valley (Su's console revenue was exactly that bridge)

dead_bodies.name: Intel (as living cautionary)
dead_bodies.why: The incumbent that lost by NOT betting: hedged architectures, kept fabs, missed mobile AND AI, process stumbles 10nm/7nm — the anti-Zen: every option kept open, every battle lost

dead_bodies.name: Opteron-era AMD (internal ghost)
dead_bodies.why: AMD had beaten Intel before (2003-06) and squandered it via the ATI debt and fab burden — the institutional memory that shaped the fabless, focused, no-hedge 2014 rebuild

lessons: Turnarounds are architecture bets with cash bridges: Zen + console revenue — the bet AND the survival mechanism designed together
Chiplets: when you can't outspend, change the yield math — economics embedded in architecture
The Intel counterfactual is the registry's cleanest lesson: hedging every option is the riskiest position
Second-source dynamics: at sufficient buyer scale, customers will finance your existence — position as the credible alternative and wait for monopsony fear

people.title: Jim Keller
people.domain: Chip architecture / Human capital as the slow variable

stake.capital: Career repeatedly staked on 'the architecture is wrong, let me rebuild it'

bets.consensus_delta: At each stop the consensus was institutional: the existing roadmap is fine. His pattern: it isn't, and the rebuild takes fewer people than you think
bets.enabler: DEC Alpha training (the finest CPU school ever assembled, dissolved into the industry when DEC died)
bets.thesis: Small senior teams rebuilding from first principles beat large teams iterating on legacy
bets.outcome: AMD K7/K8 (Athlon/Opteron — first to beat Intel, x86-64 ISA that Intel was forced to adopt), Apple A4/A5 (iPhone silicon foundation), AMD Zen (with Su), Tesla FSD chip, Intel (left when politics beat physics), Tenstorrent CEO
bets.survival_mechanism: Reputation portable across institutions — the only chip architect whose personal brand exceeds every employer's

stake.capital: Left AMD mid-Zen-glory for Tesla autopilot hardware — a car company with no silicon team
stake.reputation: staked on automotive silicon being a real category

bets.consensus_delta: Car companies buy chips, they don't design them — Mobileye/NVIDIA had the market. The bet: vertical integration would reach silicon, and inference-per-watt would be the binding constraint
bets.enabler: Musk's appetite for vertical integration; NPU design space wide open in 2016
bets.thesis: Custom inference silicon at the edge beats general-purpose — design for the exact workload
bets.outcome: Tesla FSD chip (2019): 21x Mobileye performance claim at launch; validated the custom-AI-silicon path every hyperscaler then followed
bets.survival_mechanism: Two-year project scope — in, build, ship, out before institutional decay

stake.capital: CEO of Tenstorrent — first time running the company, not the design
stake.reputation: all of it, in the hardest market (NVIDIA's shadow)

bets.consensus_delta: AI-chip startups were already a graveyard by 2021 (Wave dead, Graphcore dying). The bet: RISC-V openness + licensing model + chiplets = survive where closed full-stack challengers died
bets.enabler: RISC-V maturity; Samsung/Hyundai/Bezos capital ($700M+ raised, ~$2.6B valuation 2024, cf:md); sovereign-AI customers wanting non-NVIDIA, non-US-locked options
bets.thesis: Sell licensable, open AI compute building blocks — be the Arm of AI silicon, not the next NVIDIA
bets.outcome: Open: design wins in Japan (Rapidus/LSTC), automotive, edge (cf:md); the licensing model is the differentiated survival path
bets.survival_mechanism: IP licensing revenue arrives before silicon volume — the model funds the assault

chain: DEC Alpha (the school) → AMD K7/K8: x86-64 — Intel forced to adopt AMD's ISA, the deepest humiliation available

Tesla 2016-18: FSD chip — proved custom edge-AI silicon; every hyperscaler's custom-chip program follows the template
Intel 2018-20: the one that didn't work — politics as the un-debuggable system; left
Tenstorrent 2021→: RISC-V licensing model; the architect finally owns the company
The meta-pattern: institutions decay, craft compounds — he is the control group proving talent, not org, was the variable

dead_bodies.why: His alma mater: the best CPU team ever assembled, killed by corporate strategy failure above the engineering line — the original lesson that institutions, not architectures, are the mortal layer

people.title: Alexandr Wang

stake.capital: Dropped MIT at 19 for Scale AI (YC S16)
stake.reputation: teenage dropout in an unglamorous niche

bets.consensus_delta: 2016: everyone wanted to BUILD models; labeling training data was considered BPO work with an API — low-status, low-moat outsourcing
bets.enabler: Deep learning's data hunger compounding; autonomous-vehicle boom needing millions of labeled frames; later: RLHF making human feedback THE bottleneck
bets.thesis: Every AI wave, whatever wins, needs labeled data — sell the picks and shovels to all sides
bets.outcome: Scale rode three successive waves with the same infrastructure: AV labeling → government/defense data → RLHF for frontier labs. Revenue ~$870M (2024), valuation $14B+ (cf:md)
bets.survival_mechanism: Customer diversification across waves; when AV funding died, defense and RLHF were already ramping

stake.capital: Pushed Scale into defense contracts when tech employees were walking out over Pentagon work
stake.reputation: contrarian-patriot positioning in an industry allergic to it

bets.consensus_delta: Post-Project-Maven walkouts (Google 2018), defense AI was radioactive in the Valley. Wang went toward it loudly — op-eds, DC testimony, the works
bets.enabler: The Thiel/Palantir playbook, re-run a generation later: unfashionable customer, structural mispricing
bets.thesis: US-China AI competition makes government the most durable customer; first-mover in trust wins decade-long contracts
bets.outcome: CDAO contracts, defense revenue line; positioned Wang as the Valley's China-hawk wunderkind — the political capital later relevant to the Meta deal's regulatory shape
bets.survival_mechanism: Commercial RLHF revenue meant defense was optionality, not dependence

stake.capital: Sold 49% of Scale to Meta for $14.3B; became Meta's Chief AI Officer running Superintelligence Labs at 28
stake.reputation: from founder to the most expensive hire in history

bets.consensus_delta: Founders don't abandon their company at peak for a job. But the structure (49% non-control investment + talent extraction) was the acquihire-wave apex: Meta paid $14.3B principally for one person and his network
bets.enabler: Meta's Llama-4 stumble + Zuckerberg's panic-mode talent raid; antitrust environment making outright acquisition impossible — the 49% structure IS the regulatory workaround
bets.thesis: Frontier-lab compute + Meta's distribution beats independent data-infra ownership; his marginal impact is higher allocating $100B than running labeling ops
bets.outcome: Open. Scale's neutrality died same week (Google/OpenAI cut ties as customers) — the cost of the trade priced immediately
bets.survival_mechanism: The $14.3B de-risked everything personally; Scale continues under new CEO with Meta capital

chain: MIT dropout at 19 → YC → labeling for the AV boom: picks-and-shovels while everyone chased models
AV winter → pivot to government + RLHF: same infrastructure, new waves — the diversification that survived
RLHF era: human feedback becomes THE frontier bottleneck; Scale sits on the choke point
Defense positioning 2020→: the Thiel playbook re-run; DC credibility as moat
Jun 2025: Meta $14.3B for 49% + Wang as Chief AI Officer — the acquihire wave's apex specimen
Scale's customer neutrality dies on announcement (OpenAI/Google exit) — proof the asset was always the position between labs, and the position was sold

Personal: ~$3B+ from the transaction at 28 (cf:md)
Meta's frontier results under his org: pending — the entire bet still open

dead_bodies.name: Appen
dead_bodies.why: The Australian incumbent that owned labeling before Scale: -95% from peak as Scale ate the market — incumbency in services is a depreciating asset when the customer's needs change waves

dead_bodies.name: Mighty AI
dead_bodies.why: Scale's direct AV-labeling competitor; sold to Uber for parts 2019 — single-wave dependence (AV only) where Scale diversified

dead_bodies.name: Figure Eight (CrowdFlower)
dead_bodies.why: The labeling pioneer, sold to Appen 2019 for a fraction of past valuation — first mover in the right market, exhausted before the wave that mattered (RLHF) arrived

lessons: Picks-and-shovels with wave-diversification: the same infrastructure sold to AV, defense, and RLHF — the wedge survives wave death only if the customer base rotates
The unfashionable-customer playbook (defense) is repeatable: Thiel 2003 → Wang 2020 — each generation's allergy is the next's arbitrage
The 49% structure is the antitrust-era acquisition: study it — non-control investment + talent extraction + commercial agreement = merger without merger review
Neutrality is an asset that dies instantly on alignment: Scale's inter-lab position was worth more than its operations, and selling it ended it — price your neutrality before someone else does
people.body:
MIT dropout at 19 → YC → labeling for the AV boom: picks-and-shovels while everyone chased models
AV winter → pivot to government + RLHF: same infrastructure, new waves — the diversification that survived
RLHF era: human feedback becomes THE frontier bottleneck; Scale sits on the choke point
Defense positioning 2020→: the Thiel playbook re-run; DC credibility as moat
Jun 2025: Meta $14.3B for 49% + Wang as Chief AI Officer — the acquihire wave's apex specimen
Scale's customer neutrality dies on announcement (OpenAI/Google exit) — proof the asset was always the position between labs, and the position was sold

people.title: Pieter Levels

stake.capital: Zero — a public challenge: 12 startups in 12 months, solo, no funding
stake.reputation: built the audience BY making the bet public

bets.consensus_delta: 2014 startup orthodoxy: raise VC, hire, scale, exit. A solo founder shipping monthly with no funding was considered a hobbyist, definitionally not a 'real' startup
bets.enabler: Stripe + AWS + remote-work emergence: the full company stack operable by one person for $100/mo
bets.thesis: Most startups die of costs, not competition; zero burn + many shots + public building = positive expectancy without venture
bets.outcome: Nomad List + Remote OK emerged from the 12; most others killed fast and cheap — the point. ~$3M+/yr revenue, zero employees, ever (cf:md)
bets.survival_mechanism: Zero burn = infinite runway; killing products in weeks costs nothing; the audience compounds across products

stake.capital: Shipped PhotoAI/InteriorAI within weeks of Stable Diffusion's release

bets.consensus_delta: Mild — but speed was the edge: he shipped paid AI products while funded teams were still hiring; the wrapper window's first profitable occupant
bets.enabler: Stable Diffusion open weights (Aug 2022) + his existing 500K+ audience = zero-CAC product launches in days
bets.thesis: New model capability × existing audience × days-to-ship = window profits before competition exists
bets.outcome: PhotoAI ~$1-2M/yr at peak (cf:md); the live proof-of-concept for the entire wrapper window — and for its decay (revenue declining as platforms absorbed the feature, openly published)
bets.survival_mechanism: Portfolio rotation: when a product decays he ships the next; no single point of failure

chain: 2014: '12 startups in 12 months' public challenge → audience compounds with each ship
Nomad List/Remote OK survive the cull → ~$3M/yr solo, the existence proof
Build-in-public becomes a movement: thousands copy the model — he IS the meta-leverage-shift node
Aug 2022 Stable Diffusion → PhotoAI in weeks: first profitable occupant of the wrapper window
Publishes revenue dashboards including the DECLINE — the only honest decay data in the window
2024→: the model he pioneered (solo + AI tools + audience) becomes the default path for a generation of builders priced out of venture

facts: ~$3M+/yr across portfolio, still zero employees (cf:md, self-reported but consistently verified by payment-processor screenshots)
X audience >600K — the distribution asset underneath everything (cf:md)
Publicly documents product revenue decay as platforms absorb features — the honest wrapper-window dataset
The archetype cited in every individual-leverage analysis, including this tool's meta-leverage-shift trend

future_paths.falsifier: All products remain independent through 2028

future_paths.falsifier: Audience-less solo products demonstrably succeed at scale in 2026-27 cohorts

dead_bodies.name: Most of his own 12 startups
dead_bodies.why: Killed publicly and cheaply — the model's feature, not failure: cheap kills are the tuition the venture path pays in years and dilution

dead_bodies.name: VC-funded nomad/remote plays (Teleport, Roam Research-adjacent co-living)
dead_bodies.why: Raised millions to attack markets Nomad List served solo at profit — burn rate as the cause of death in markets too small for venture but perfect for one person

dead_bodies.name: The funded wrapper cohort 2023-24
dead_bodies.why: Raised seed rounds to build what he shipped in weeks; CAC + burn + platform absorption killed them while his zero-cost versions decayed gracefully into the next product

lessons: Zero burn is a strategy, not a limitation: infinite runway converts windows too small for venture into personal fortunes
Audience-before-product inverts CAC to zero: build-in-public is distribution infrastructure, built once, reused every launch
Kill speed is the hidden variable: his portfolio expectancy comes from cheap deaths, not big wins
He published the decay data: the wrapper window's only honest revenue curves came from the guy with nothing to hide — transparency as both marketing and dataset
